name: StartupBase
description: An ongoing startup discovery platform and launch directory where founders list their products for early adopters and feedback. The audience is founders, makers and people hunting for new startups.

auth.notes: Register with email/password (social login commonly offered). A free listing is permanent; a Pro/featured tier adds placement, a do-follow link and a badge.

best_practices: Treat the listing as evergreen, not a one-day spike — write copy that still reads well months from now.
Make the tagline specific and outcome-focused so it stands out when browsers scan the discovery feed.
Fill out every optional field (categories, tags, social) — completeness improves how the listing ranks and gets surfaced.
Pick the most accurate primary category so the right early-adopter audience finds you.
Use a clean square logo that is legible at small sizes across the directory.
Write a benefit-led description that names the problem and the user, then keep your profile updated as the product evolves.

questions.label: Tagline / pitch
questions.help: One sentence: what it is and who it's for.

questions.label: Description
questions.help: What the product does, the problem it solves, and who it's for.

questions.label: Category
questions.help: Pick the primary category that best fits.
